message:
fix(sync): reject duplicate issue IDs across all JSONL ingest paths

Several code paths that read `.beads/issues.jsonl` previously collapsed
duplicate issue-ID records silently by overwriting earlier entries with
later ones. That's a classic "last-write-wins" data-loss footgun: an
operator who accidentally appends a second record for `bd-123` has no
way to know that their earlier row was just erased, and a DB rebuild
from such a JSONL then propagates the silent loss into SQLite.

Harden four distinct ingest surfaces to turn duplicates into an
explicit, actionable error:

- `validate_jsonl_issue_records` (doctor / preflight): now records a
  per-line `Duplicate issue id '<id>'` failure and keeps scanning so
  operators see every duplicate in one pass.
- `analyze_jsonl` (orphan-cleanup precomputation): now returns
  `BeadsError::Config` with the offending id + path + line number.
- `read_issues_from_jsonl` (merge pipeline input): same error class as
  `analyze_jsonl`; prevents `execute_merge` from silently seeing only
  one side of a duplicate pair.
- `import_from_jsonl` (force/rebuild imports): previously this path
  emitted a `tracing::warn!` and overwrote `issues[index]`; now it
  hard-fails with a Config error so `sync --force`/`--rebuild` refuses
  to proceed against a JSONL containing duplicate ids.

Tests added:
- test_analyze_jsonl_rejects_duplicate_issue_ids
- test_import_duplicate_issue_ids_error
- test_validate_jsonl_issue_records_rejects_duplicate_issue_ids
- test_preflight_import_rejects_duplicate_issue_ids_during_validation

Also update the existing invalid-lines test to use two distinct ids so
the "good lines" still parse under the new duplicate check.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
